title: Preference-aware filters and visual chips on the week roll-up
description: Let captains filter and sort the matrix by 'My Tennis' data — handedness, court side, match-type appetite, competitiveness, open-to-fill-in. Adds visual chips per player so at-a-glance scanning picks out the right combinations without hovering every cell.
acceptance_criteria: Filter panel visible alongside the matrix — sticky on scroll on large screens
Filters: match-type appetite (mixed / same-gender / open-to-fill-in), handedness, preferred court side, competitiveness range, open-to-fill-in boolean
Filtered matrix updates via HTMX without full reload
Each player row shows small preference chips next to their name: handedness (R/L), court side (D/A/E), competitiveness (1-5 dots), open-to-fill-in (star)
Chip hover reveals the full label
Filter state persists in URL query string
testing_requirements: Integration test: filter argument narrows the returned player set correctly

context.user_story: As a captain, I want to quickly isolate 'available left-handers who love mixed and are competitive' so I can put the right partnership together.
